mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.41% as of 2016-11-01 indicates near-zero rates post-crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer confidence and spending, translating into positive equity returns within 5 trading days as forward-looking investors price in sustained demand.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,128 thousand as of 2016-11-01 indicates strong labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth below 2% signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,712.5 billion (2017 dollars) as of 2016-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production combined with rising core inflation signals stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and increases cost of capital, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change < -0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.86 (2017=100) and CPIAUCSL at 242.35 as of 2016-11-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in retail sales combined with rising unemployment signals deteriorating consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity multiples contract.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465,513 million and UNRATE at 4.6% as of 2016-11-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2-month increase in housing starts above 1,200 thousand units signals strengthening residential investment and forward-looking consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors expand.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,323 thousand as of 2016-10-01 indicates strong housing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% combined with accelerating wage growth (proxied by payroll expansion) signals tight labor market conditions that increase corporate wage costs and reduce profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS monthly change > 200,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 145,128 thousand as of 2016-11-01
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% in a tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in core inflation above 0.3% signals persistent price pressure that forces the Fed to maintain or accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.41% and CPIAUCSL at 242.35 as of 2016-11-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.
